data.title: U.S. strategy is said to take aim at Venezuela’s ghost fleet of oil tankers
data.description:
The United States has begun aggressively pursuing oil tankers operating near Venezuela as part of a new legal strategy aimed at confiscating vessels involved in the global trade of sanctioned oil, The Wall Street Journal reported Tuesday, citing U.S. Justice Department officials.

The approach was on display in recent days when the U.S. Coast Guard chased the Bella 1, a sanctioned tanker whose crew reportedly refused to allow boarding. The Bella 1 marks the third vessel targeted after U.S. authorities took control of two other large crude carriers, the Skipper and the Centuries.

Unlike those earlier ships, which were carrying close to 2 million barrels of Venezuelan crude when boarded, shipping analysts at Kpler said the Bella 1 was likely not carrying oil when the pursuit began. The firm noted that shortly after the Skipper was seized, the Bella 1 initially altered course away from Venezuela before later reversing direction back toward the country.

U.S. officials said the effort represents a shift beyond simply seizing sanctioned cargo. Authorities are now focusing on dismantling the so-called “ghost fleet” of tankers that facilitate illicit oil trading worldwide.

Venezuela has condemned the actions, characterizing them as outright theft and accusing Washington of piracy. Officials in Caracas said the Trump administration was attempting to appropriate Venezuelan oil and strip the country of its energy resources.

Expedited seizure warrants

At the center of the operation is a specialized Threat Finance Unit within the National Security Section of the U.S. Attorney’s Office for the District of Columbia. According to the office’s leadership, the unit handles these cases because of both its expertise in financial threats and jurisdictional authority. The pace and scale of tanker seizures have increased sharply this year as part of a broader security push under President Donald Trump.

Officials said the unit has pursued similar cases in the past, but recent resources devoted to tracking tankers have significantly accelerated the process. Investigators can now assemble the evidence needed to request a seizure warrant within weeks, rather than months.

As more warrants move through the courts, the crackdown threatens to sharply curtail Venezuelan crude exports. It could also disrupt oil flows to countries that rely on the shadow tanker network, including Cuba, Iran, Russia and China.

U.S. confiscates oil, ships

Speaking to reporters on Monday, Trump said the U.S. intends to retain both the seized oil and the vessels themselves, citing the deployment of an unprecedented maritime presence in the region.

Operationally, the Coast Guard has led the actions, underscoring that the campaign is being framed as law enforcement rather than a military blockade. Videos shared publicly by Attorney General Pam Bondi and Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem show personnel descending by rope from helicopters onto tanker decks.

Former Coast Guard chief counsel William Baumgartner said the service maintains specialized teams trained to board and secure vessels of this kind. Under international maritime law, he explained, ships can be stopped and searched to determine compliance with U.S. regulations, particularly if they are deemed stateless or operating under false flags.

In the case of the Skipper, which was boarded on Dec. 10, U.S. officials said authorities executed a seizure warrant and are now moving through legal proceedings to retain the cargo. A Justice Department spokesperson declined to comment on whether similar warrants had been issued for the Centuries or the Bella 1, noting that such orders are often sealed until enforcement actions occur. The Centuries was taken into U.S. control on Saturday.

The tanker seizures strike directly at the financial foundation of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro, whose government depends heavily on oil revenue. Roughly 70% of the country’s crude exports move through a fleet of sanctioned vessels now increasingly in the U.S. crosshairs.

Trump has also ordered a blockade of sanctioned oil tankers entering and leaving Venezuela, a step that represents a significant escalation in Washington’s pressure campaign against the Maduro government, the Journal reported.

data.title: Tencent-backed MiniMax unveils 'significantly enhanced' M2.1 model
data.description:
MiniMax, a Chinese rival to OpenAI (OPENAI), unveiled its new M2.1 artificial intelligence model on Tuesday, stating it is “significantly enhanced” over previous offerings.

“In M2.1, we are committed to improving performance in real-world complex tasks: focusing particularly on usability across more programming languages and office scenarios, and achieving the best level in this domain,” the Tencent (TCEHY) backed company wrote in a blog post.

Some of the highlights of the new model include “exceptional” multi-programming language capabilities, as M2.1 is now enhanced for developer languages such as rust, Java, Golang, C++, Kotlin, Objective-C, TypeScript, JavaScript, and others. MiniMax also said that it had boosted the Android and iOS development capabilities in M2.1, hoping to stem what the industry sees as a “widely recognized weakness in mobile development.” 

The new model also offers more complete composite instruction constraints, “more concise and efficient responses,” compared to the previous M2 model; increased agent and tool scaffolding generalization capabilities; and better quality dialogue and writing.

Earlier this week, MiniMax and rival Zhipu AI provided a first glimpse into their business models and earnings potential through listing filings. Zhipu and MiniMax generated 312.4M yuan ($44.4M) and $30.5M in revenue, respectively.

By comparison, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said last month that the company's revenue run-rate is north of $20B and could hit “hundreds of billions” by 2030.

data.title: Whole Foods takes its new urban grocery store concept to New Jersey
data.description:
Amazon's (AMZN) Whole Foods Market opened a new style of store in New Jersey that could be part of a bigger rollout.

Whole Foods Market's new Daily Shop in Hoboken is described as a small-format, convenience-oriented version of a traditional Whole Foods, focused on grab-and-go, essentials, and local items for dense urban neighborhoods. The store is significantly smaller than a conventional Whole Foods at around 4,800 to 5,000 square feet versus roughly 40,000 square feet for a typical location. The smaller size allows it to slot into high-traffic corridors and serve as a quick-shop option, prioritizing grab-and-go meals, snacks, prepared foods, produce, meat and seafood, bakery items, alcohol, everyday essentials, and health-and-wellness products. Whole Foods' 365 private-label line will also have a strong presence at the store. 

"Opening our first store in Hoboken is an exciting milestone, and we’re grateful for the warm welcome from the neighborhood," stated Victor Luciano, Store Team Leader for Whole Foods Market Daily Shop. "We look forward to providing the community with great service, grocery essentials that meet our high quality standards, and quick grab-and-go meals to make their lives easier," added Luciano.

Strategically, the store acts as an infill complement to nearby full-size Whole Foods locations in Jersey City and Weehawken, extending the banner’s reach without the capital intensity or footprint requirements of a traditional supermarket. Importantly, Whole Foods is not treating Hoboken as a one-off pilot of the concept. Instead, Daily Shop is expected to be part of a deliberate multi-unit rollout. The Hoboken location follows several Daily Shop openings in Manhattan and is described as the fifth store under the new banner.

Founded in 1980, Austin-based Whole Foods Market is the world’s leading natural and organic foods retailer and the first certified organic national grocer in the United States. Part of Amazon’s Worldwide Grocery Stores, Whole Foods Market serves customers in more than 545 stores across the U.S., Canada, and the U.K. Amazon (AMZN) acquired Whole Foods Market in 2017 for $13.7 billion.

Whole Foods Market has the highest degree of customer overlap with Trader Joe's, Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM), and The Fresh Market, as well as larger grocery chains owned by Kroger (KR) and Albertsons' (ACI).

data.title: Japan's finance minister signals readiness for yen intervention
data.description:
Japan has a "free hand" in dealing with the yen's (JPY:USD) recent depreciation, Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said on Tuesday, signaling Tokyo's readiness to intervene and address the currency's sharp decline.

"I do not believe that the overall movements reflect the fundamentals at all," Katayama said in response to a reporter's question on the yen's decline.

She said the government will respond to excessive movements, including speculative moves, adding that she has a "free hand."

Hiroyuki Machida, director of Japan FX and commodities sales at ANZ, told Reuters that the yen's recent weakness reflects the government's reflationary fiscal policies and the Bank of Japan's still-easy monetary policy.

He added that the market needs to see further monetary tightening for a correction in the yen's weakness, in light of next fiscal year's expansionary budget.

data.title: Holiday spending up solidly in the age of AI - Visa
data.description:
Visa (V) released its U.S. holiday spending report on Tuesday. Preliminary data from Visa (V) showed that overall holiday retail spending increased 4.2% year over year across all payment types, including cash and check. 

Online retail spending rose 7.8%, driven by early-season promotions and convenience. On a global basis, markets outside the U.S. saw seasonal spending rise at a solid pace, including in Australia (+5.0%), Canada (+4.4%), South Africa (+7.9%), and the U.K. (+3.6%).

By category, electronics sales jumped 5.8%, fueled by demand for high-performance devices in the AI era. That tally could be a positive sign for Best Buy (BBY). The read was not as positive for the home improvement sector, as building materials and garden equipment sales decreased by 1.0%, which does not bode well for Home Depot (HD) and Lowe's (LOW).

"Whether shoppers were upgrading their tech, refreshing their closets, or stocking up at one-stop shops, retailers delivered seamless shopping experiences both in stores and online," stated Visa (V) Chief Economist Wayne Best. "This season also marked a turning point, with artificial intelligence shaping how people discover products, compare prices, and interact with offers. This led to a more informed, more intentional consumer, ensuring they could stretch their discretionary spending," added Best.

data.title: Lilly-backed radiophama startup Atkis seeks U.S. IPO
data.description:
Aktis Oncology (AKTS), a radiopharmaceutical drug developer backed by pharma giants such as Eli Lilly (LLY), has filed its preliminary regulatory filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission for an initial public offering in the U.S.

Boston, Massachusetts-headquartered Aktis (AKTS), whose lead product candidate, [225Ac]Ac-AKY-1189, is currently undergoing early-stage development, raised $175M from a Series B funding round led by RA Capital Management last year.

The financing, backed by Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY), Eli Lilly (LLY), and Merck (MRK), among others, implied a purchase price of $4.00 apiece for 43.75M shares of Atkis' (AKTS) preferred stock.

According to an S-1 filing with the SEC on Friday, the clinical-stage biotech has recorded significant losses since its founding in 2020. Its upcoming catalysts include initial results from a Part-1 dose escalation portion of a Phase 1b trial for [225Ac]Ac-AKY-1189 in urothelial cancer and other Nectin-4 expressing tumors.

Atkis (AKTS) recorded $4.6M and $1.5M in revenue during the first nine months of 2025 and throughout last year, respectively, thanks to a collaboration agreement it signed with Eli Lilly (LLY) in early 2024 for cancer-targeting radiopharmaceutical drugs.

As part of the deal, the Indiana-based pharma giant agreed to hand the company $60M upfront in addition to potential milestone payments worth up to $1.1B.

data.title: Copper prices reach $12,000 for first time amid tariff disruptions
data.description:
Copper prices surged to a record above $12,000 a metric ton, propelled by a combination of supply disruptions at key mines and sharp distortions in global trade flows tied to U.S. tariff policy under President Donald Trump. The rally puts the metal on track for its strongest annual performance since the aftermath of the global financial crisis in 2009.

On the London Metal Exchange, copper climbed as much as 1% to an intraday peak of $12,044 a ton, extending a run that has pushed prices more than 30% higher this year. Concerns that the U.S. could impose tariffs on copper have played a pivotal role, prompting a wave of imports into the country and leaving buyers elsewhere scrambling to secure material.

The resulting shift in trade patterns has been so pronounced that prices have continued to rise despite weakening demand fundamentals in China, which accounts for roughly half of global copper consumption.

Although the metal is often seen as a gauge of worldwide industrial health, China’s slowdown has failed to halt the rally. Many traders now expect prices to continue climbing as shipments into the U.S. accelerate in anticipation of possible tariffs, Bloomberg News reported.

Supply-side pressures have added further momentum. Production interruptions at mines across the Americas, Africa and Asia have heightened concerns that the market is approaching a significant shortfall. Deutsche Bank has warned that output from the world’s largest copper producers is likely to decline about 3% this year, with the risk of further reductions in 2026.

Inventories remain adequate for the moment, but analysts at Morgan Stanley argue that the balance will deteriorate sharply next year. The bank forecasts that global demand could exceed supply by roughly 600,000 tons in 2026, marking the most severe deficit in more than two decades, with gaps expected to widen thereafter.

Longstanding concerns about supply constraints have been a recurring theme in bullish outlooks for copper, alongside expectations of rising demand from electric vehicles, renewable energy infrastructure and artificial intelligence-related applications. Citigroup has told clients that prices could climb as high as $15,000 a ton in an optimistic scenario, particularly if a softer U.S. dollar and lower interest rates increase copper’s appeal to investors.

Still, the surge has its skeptics. During the pandemic-era rally, copper prices ultimately stalled below $11,000 a ton as resistance from Chinese buyers intensified. This time, too, some analysts are urging caution. Goldman Sachs has noted that the recent gains have been driven largely by investor positioning around future tightness rather than immediate supply-and-demand conditions.

Even so, the bank continues to favor copper among industrial metals and recently raised its price projection for next year to $11,400 a ton.

As of late morning in London, copper was trading around $12,042.50 a ton on the LME. Other base metals also advanced, with nickel posting gains of roughly 2%.

data.title: Six Flags plans to open its first theme park outside of North America next week
data.description:
Six Flags Entertainment Corporation (FUN) is scheduled to open and operate its first theme park outside the U.S. next week in a notable development for the travel and leisure sector.

Six Flags Qiddiya City in Saudi Arabia will open on December 31 as the flagship theme park of Saudi Arabia's Qiddiya City giga-project. The opening will position the theme park at the center of the Qiddiya City project, which is seen as both a tourism catalyst and a symbolic milestone for Saudi Vision 2030’s push into large-scale entertainment and leisure. 

Six Flags (FUN) does not own Six Flags Qiddiya City, as the project is owned and funded by Qiddiya Investment Company, which is backed by Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund. For its part, Six Flags (FUN) is engaged under a management/services and licensing agreement to operate the park and provide its brand and expertise.

The theme park plans to welcome guests with a New Year's Eve launch that effectively rings in 2026 for Saudi Arabia’s emerging entertainment hub near Riyadh, located in the Tuwaiq Mountains about 40 minutes from the capital as part of a broader mixed-use destination combining entertainment, sports, culture, and residential development. At opening, Six Flags Qiddiya City will feature 28 rides and attractions across more than 320,000 square meters, including 10 major thrill rides and various family-focused experiences, with headline draws such as Falcons Flight, billed as the world’s tallest, fastest, and longest coaster, alongside Iron Rattler, promoted as the tallest tilt coaster, and Spitfire, described as the tallest inverted coaster, cementing the park’s ambition to be a global thrill destination. The attractions are organized into themed lands such as Steam Town, Twilight Gardens, Discovery Springs, City Thrills, Valley of Fortune, and Grand Exposition/City of Thrills, blending high-intensity rides with immersive environments.

The opening is significant because it marks Six Flags' (FUN) first from-the-ground-up international flagship developed in partnership with a sovereign-backed entity. Analysts have noted that the opening marks a strategic pivot by Six Flags (FUN) toward management and brand-licensing models in high-growth markets. Theme park industry observers call Six Flags Qiddiya City one of the most anticipated global theme park openings of the decade.

Shares of Six Flags (FUN) are down more than 66% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Hyperscale Data’s bitcoin holdings reach 514.97 BTC, exceeding 100% of market capitalization
data.description:
• Hyperscale Data (GPUS) Tuesday said that its Bitcoin treasury, including current holdings and cash allocated to committed purchases of Bitcoin, totaled approximately $76 million, based on the price of Bitcoin as of December 21.
  • This amount represents ~100.75% of the company's market capitalization, based on the company's stock price at the close of trading on December 22, 2025. 
  • Hyperscale Data's (GPUS) next goal is to reach $100 million in Bitcoin on its balance sheet.

data.title: Delta plans to become the first U.S. carrier with a direct flight to Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL) plans to launch nonstop service between Atlanta and Riyadh on October 23, 2026, using an Airbus A350-900. The new route will make Delta (DAL) the only U.S. carrier offering direct flights to Saudi Arabia and mark a major step in its strategy to tap fast-growing Middle East demand. 

The Riyadh flight will operate initially daily around launch before transitioning to three times weekly from early November, with block times of roughly 13 hours to Riyadh and about 15.5 hours back to Atlanta, which will make it among Delta's (DAL) longest routes. Atlanta’s role as Delta’s largest hub allows the carrier to funnel extensive domestic and Latin American feed into the service, while Riyadh’s King Khalid International Airport is being developed as a global hub under Saudi Arabia’s Vision 2030, which aims to diversify the economy through tourism, aviation, and investment. The route is reported to be supported by significant government subsidies aimed at positioning Riyadh as a premier global aviation hub.

Strategically, the launch is seen as significant inside the airline industry because it anchors Delta's (DAL) new exclusive partnership with start-up carrier Riyadh Air, under which Delta will be Riyadh Air’s sole North American partner and Riyadh Air will be Delta’s exclusive partner in Riyadh. The two carrier plan for codesharing, reciprocal connectivity, loyalty cooperation, and broader operational collaboration in areas such as maintenance and ground handling. The partnership creates an important hub-to-hub corridor between North America and an emerging Middle East hub, giving Delta (DAL) a proprietary access point into the region rather than relying on passengers self-connecting via Gulf carriers like Emirates or Qatar Airways.
